feed.title: SAP Community - SAP Analytics Cloud
feed.subtitle: SAP Analytics Cloud Q&A in SAP Community

entry.title: SAC - Data overlap in graphic
entry.content:
Hi all,

I have a "combination stack columnn & line" chart but I have problem of Data overlap:

I've seen I can flag the option "Avoid data overlap" but I don't want the data to disappear:

Is there a way to put the datas of the line near the "point" and not on the "point"?

entry.title: IAS to SAC: User Updates Not Provisioned
entry.content:
Hello Experts,

I have provisioned users from IAS to SAC.

However, when I change a user in IAS (e.g., updating the last name) and run the Read job, the changes are not provisioned to the target system (SAC).

There is no error, but the users are read, and no update is performed in the target system.

What should I pay attention to and what needs to be configured to ensure that only the user changes are successfully provisioned?

 

Many Thanks

Best regard

entry.title: Labelling length of dimensions in the bar chart
entry.content:
In a table: I can change the label length from short to medium length.

I can't find this option in the bar chart. Is it not possible to adjust the label length in the bar chart?

Short and medium-length text is maintained in the source system.

entry.title: Parametrization Variables in SAC
entry.content:
Hello,

I have an example that includes the forecast of students enrolled in courses, such as bachelor's, master's, and postgraduate programs. Each level has a price, for instance, bachelor's €300, master's €1,000, and postgraduate €800. What I want is to make these prices configurable parameters, meaning I want to create two tables: one with the necessary information and another table containing these variables. This way, when the user wants to change the prices of higher education levels, the calculations will be updated automatically.

thanks in advance,

Ganjos

entry.title: Allocation in SAC
entry.content:
Hello,

I have a scenario that I need help with. I upload data with a split on the members of [d/COUNTRY] and on [d/STORE] = "#". The same total is uploaded afterwords, but the other way around i.e. with a split on the members of [d/STORE] and [d/COUNTRY = "#". I now need to merge these two together with a Data Action while having almost the same original split on [d/COUNTRY] and [d/STORE].

Anyone have any ideas?

entry.title: SAC VDT
entry.content:
Here’s a rephrased version of your message:

Hello,

I’m encountering an issue in the SAC system with the VDT drill-into feature. After using drill-down, I’m unable to retain the original structure that I had before drilling in, and the hierarchy gets disconnected from the VDT.

I would like to know if there’s any option to preserve the initial structure.

Thanks in advance.

This version clarifies the issue and maintains a professional tone.

entry.title: How to Display Data Labels Inside Columns/Bars in SAC Story Charts
entry.content:
Hi SAP Community,

I’m using a Bar/Column chart in SAP Analytics Cloud (SAC) and would like to display the data labels inside the columns or bars, rather than outside or on top of them. I can’t find an option to do this in the settings.

Can someone help me with this? Is there a way to display the data labels inside the bars in SAC?

Current Design in SAC

the Expected Design

entry.title: Turning off notifications for Validation Rules updates
entry.content:
Hello SAC Community,

for some time I have been meaning to ask you, if any of you know, if there is a possibility in SAC, to turn off notifications that you receive from Validation Rules everytime there is an update in the given Dimension. We have a planning application that allows users to create new members in a Dimension. That Dimension is included in a Validation Rule in the model and everytime we create a new member in the Dimension, we receive the following notification. It is pretty annoying because we create upto 20 new dimension members in one go which creates 20 notifications. We have not found a way to turn these notifications off. Did any of you?

Thank you very much!

Adam Vacek Nekola

entry.title: Dynamic YoY Story Calculation - SAC
entry.content:
Hello,
I need to calculate YoY for YTD, QTD, and MTD. Since my reports are dynamic, it's essential to display the period at the top of the table. This makes it impractical to create restricted calculations like "QTD Current" & "QTD Previous."

I’ve used an advanced filter to set up a dynamic period (see below), but I’m unsure how to create a dynamic YOY comparison (restricted?) calculation. I need both a percentage and an amount difference. (Using the "YOY" formula in the measure, only works for yearly values.)

also, I don't want to use a "client calculation" on the table because it will require to recreate it every month/quarter/year as the time passes.

How can I achieve this?

Thanks

entry.title: SAC Master data entry check the existing record
entry.content:
Hi Team,

I am newbie to SAP Analytics cloud. I am learning the concepts now. I tried to do the master data entry using popup. I am trying to enter the data into a table mentioned below.

Popup screen will  be like ...

My Issue :- If Emp ID is getting populated with a value which is already there in the model then data shouldn't get loaded into the model from story.
For this i writing the code like below, where i am not able to stop the execution of the code. 

var tot_cnt = PlanningModel_1.getMembers("EMP_Details");
for (var i=0; i< tot_cnt.length ; i++){
if (INP_EMP_Id.getValue() === tot_cnt[i].id) 
{
Application.showMessage(ApplicationMessageType.Warning,"EMP ID already exists");
}
}
 
-- Here i need to break further execution of code.
 
New_emp_id = ({id: INP_EMP_Id.getValue(),description : INP_Emp_Name.getValue(), 
hierarchies : {H1: {parentId: DD_Hierarchy.getSelectedKey()}},
properties:{Region: DD_Region.getSelectedKey(), Skillset: DD_Skillset.getSelectedKey(), Expertise: DD_Expertise.getSelectedKey(), Joining_Date: INP_DOJ.getValue()}  });
 
 
PlanningModel_1.createMembers("EMP_Details",New_emp_id);
//Application.refreshData();
Table_1.getDataSource().refreshData();
Application.showMessage(ApplicationMessageType.Success,"Employee added successfully");

Can anyone of you please help me on this.

Thanks,
Subash

entry.title: Hello, Comment function is disabled in the SAC Story, Datasource is BW Query
entry.content:
Hello, Comment function is disabled in the SAC Story, Datasource is BW Query.

is this issue is at the query level or the authorisation level for the SAC ? Please advise.

entry.title: CalendarIntegration status refresh issue on Sap Analytics Cloud
entry.content:
Hello everyone,

I noticed that CalendarIntegration.getCalendarEventById(<ID>).getStatus() returns the event status as it was at the time the page was loaded. If the status of that event changes between the page load and the execution of the script CalendarIntegration.getCalendarEventById(SV_Sheet).getStatus(), the output remains the same. Is there a way to refresh the CalendarIntegration, or is it necessary to force a page refresh?

entry.title: Pie Chart using R visualization in SAP Analytics Cloud
entry.content:
Hi,

In SAP Analytics Cloud, traditional charts are highly effective for most use cases. However, there are times when we need to push the boundaries and create something truly unique. This is where custom R visualizations come into play, offering a powerful way to go beyond standard capabilities.

Our goal was straightforward: we wanted to create a clean, interactive pie chart without the traditional lines pointing to the values, while also enhancing the overall presentation.

In this blog, we’ll walk you through the process of designing an interactive pie chart using R visualizations in SAP Analytics Cloud

Step 1: Creating a Widget

Click on Insert->Others -> R visualization

Step 2: Choosing the model

Click on ‘Add Input data’ on the right pane and choose the model which you are going to build and the corresponding measures and dimensions and click on Ok Button.

.

Step 3: Writing the R Script

Click on ‘add script’ and you will get the below window where you can write your R scripts

Step 4: Scripting using R

Write the scripts, click on 'Execute' button to preview and finally click apply

library(plotly)

library(dplyr)

# Create the data frame

df = data.frame(

  product = MD_Test_Model$’Dimension 1’,

  val1 = MD_Test_Mode$’Test Calculation’)

#Note: After typing the model followed by $, when you press ‘Ctrl’ + Space, it lets you to choose the respective dimension/measure

# Summarize

df <- df %>%

  group_by(product) %>%

  summarise(val1 = sum(val1)) %>%

  arrange(desc(val1)) %>%

  mutate(percentage = val1 / sum(val1) * 100) # Calculate percentage for hover info

# Define custom colors for the pie chart

custom_colors <- c(

  "#1f77b4", "#ff7f0e", "#2ca02c", "#d62728", "#9467bd",

  "#8c564b", "#e377c2", "#7f7f7f", "#bcbd22", "#17becf"

)

# Creating the  pie chart

plot_ly(

  df,

  labels = ~product,

  values = ~val1,

  type = 'pie',

  textinfo = 'percent',             # Shows percentages on the pie slices

  hoverinfo = 'text',               # Customizes hover information

  text = ~paste("Value: ", val1, "<br>", "Percentage: ", round(percentage, 1), "%"), # Hover text

  marker = list(colors = custom_colors) # Applies custom colors

) %>%

  layout(

    title = "Title"

    margin=list(l=10,r=30,t=30,b=10),

    legend = list(

      orientation = "h",            # Places the legend horizontally

      x = 0.5,y=1.45,  xanchor = "center",  # Centers the legend

      font = list(size = 8        # Adjusts font size of the legend

    )

  )

The Pie Chart is Created

entry.title: Temporal allocation of values in proportion to predetermined weights in SAC
entry.content:
Good day,

I’m trying to allocate a budget over a year per historical weights with an allocation step and am encountering difficulties.

I have completed the steps enumerated in a similar previously asked question (linked below), yet when I run the allocation, the results are not allocated as expected.

Solved: Allocation of values in proportion to last year Ac... - SAP Community

 

As per the image, the current budget per equipment is entirely allocated on 2025-12-31. My goal is to distribute it according to weights from the ‘driver’ measure.

 

In the second image we can see that the budget has not been allocated as I would expect.

 

For equipment BBB, I would expect September through December to each contain 3000$ in the 'Values' measure. Additionally, my drivers have unexpectedly changed. Are my allocation rules incorrect for what I want to do?

 

Any assistance is appreciated.

entry.title: SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition and SAC import connection setting failure
entry.content:
I'm currently working on an import connection between my company's SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition and SAC.
I am proceeding with the work based on the contents of the blog below, but I am having trouble creating "Communication Arrangement".

https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-sap/sap-s-4hana-cloud-public-edition%E3%81%A8sap-analytics-cloud%E3%81%AE%E6%8E%A5%E7%B6%9A%E6%96%B9%E6%B3%95/ba-p/13569275

In particular
・New Communication System：Creation Success
System ID : SAC_INTEGRATION_0087
System Name : SAC Integration
・New Communication User：Creation Success
User Name: SAC_COMM_USER
Description: SAC Integration
・New Communication Arrangement：Creation failure
Scenario: SAP_COM_0087
Arrangement Name: SAP_COM_0087
・About the failure situation
"SAP_COM_0087" could not be selected in the New Communication Arrangement/Scenario column.
Could someone please tell me how to resolve it?

Thank you very much.

entry.title: Business Content Live model connection change
entry.content:
Hi All,

Good day!!

Requirement :  To incorporate the business content SAP Finance based on the client's S4Hana.

I am trying to download the business content of SAP Finance Live tag which has the live models.

We do have live tunnel connections established with S4 and I have already activated the Odata Services which were related the content. 

The content model has the "SAPFIN" connection live and I tried to open the model but it was not allowing to do so.

Can anyone please help on how to incorporate in the Standard story? Because it was not allowing for me to edit the story .

Kind regards,

Tha.Ram

entry.title: Excel export from another page in SAC Story
entry.content:
Hello,

I am trying to create script Button (in a Popup)  to export in Excel table from different page in my Story in SAC but the code is giving me error - the table is coming from different page.

Do you know a workaround for the script so it can take the respective table?

ExportToExcel_2.exportReport();

entry.title: SAC - Model - Account - entry for "Units & Currencies" disappears
entry.content:
Hi Community,

since we have the new update on 18.11.2024 on our tenant, we are facing this problem:

In the account dimension in the model, entries in the column "Units & Currencies" are disappearing. If I enter this information again, it will be disappear the next day. This leads to errors in the currency conversion.

Do you have any ideas?

Let me know if you need more information.

Data is coming from BW, using daily import jobs.

Kind regards,
Eric

entry.title: API Enterprise Project using SAC and S/4 Public Cloud
entry.content:
Dear experts,

We have successsfully tested the Enterprise Project API, using the Postman application. With Postman we have simulated this API and were able to create na enterprise project and its WBS Elements.

This is the API link that we've configured to simulate this connection: https://my412440-api.s4hana.cloud.sap/sap/opu/odata/sap/API_ENTERPRISE_PROJECT_SRV;v=0002/A_EnterpriseProject

To use the API we have created a communication user (TESTEAPIPROJ), a communication system (SAC_S4HANA_PROJ) and the communication arrangement (SAP_COM_0308). We have attached screenshot of the mentioned objects in the document "API Enterprise Project using SAC and S4 Public Cloud.pdf".

The final step would be to connect SAC to the API so that we can create an enterprise project in the S/4HANA Public Cloud system from SAP Analytics Cloud.

In SAC we created a connection (testeodata) with basic authentication type. The user associated to this connection is the communication user that we have used previously in this process. Also, in SAC, we cannot copy the full API link. The system only accepts until https://my412440-api.s4hana.cloud.sap/sap/opu/odata/sap/API_ENTERPRISE_PROJECT_SRV;v=0002

However, in this system, we are not able to specify the method to call the previously mentioned API.

How should we proceed to connect both systems using this API?

Best regards,

Francisco

entry.title: Date input control in the optimized view story - SAC
entry.content:
Hello, 
I want to create a date input control I used to have in the "classic story" but doesn't seems to work in the optimized view.

that's how I created the date input control:

that's the result I need (a line range) in classic story:

How ever I cannot reproduce it (or convert it) in the optimised view:

How can I do it?
